key_points: Transfer students frequently waitlisted for campus housing with no guarantee
Housing waitlist is a real risk, especially for non-freshmen
Campus construction ongoing, which may affect residential experience
key_quotes: She has been informed that she has been waitlisted for campus housing and it may not be guaranteed.
I do not live in the States and this is a huge pain in the butt.

key_points: Greek life dominates — 48% of students are in fraternities or sororites
Non-Greek students worry about feeling left out of the social scene
Fan culture is genuinely warm; rivals and visitors consistently praise TCU crowds
key_quotes: I'm worried that if I don't join a sorority I'm gonna feel kind of left out.
I've been to a football and a baseball game... both times their fans were super nice.
TCU is ranked in the real T10 for social scene and attractiveness of student body.

key_points: No strong complaints or praise about on-campus dining in student posts
Fort Worth restaurant scene near campus is widely praised by students and parents
A paid laundry service exists at $119/month — students debate if it's worth it
key_quotes: Bearded Lady, Buff Bros, Cane Rosso, and Joe T's are go-to spots near campus.
Is it worth it to pay for TCU's laundry service? The cheapest option is $119 per month.

key_points: No dedicated mental health support discussions found in student posts
One student openly discussed hospitalization for mental health affecting GPA
High financial stress around aid and debt appears frequently in student posts
key_quotes: I was hospitalized for mental health, heavily medicated, very physically ill, and switched schools twice.
The recent rejections have been hitting me hard... mental health should be my focus.

key_points: Need-aware for international students — aid denials sent within 24 hours of parent income forms
Domestic merit scholarships are real and generous, but sticker price remains very high
Students regularly warned that a $30k scholarship still leaves $50k+ gap per year
key_quotes: They straight-up told me that they didn't have enough money for me. I received my decision less than 24 hours after my mom filled out a form asking how much she would contribute.
I do want you to know there's still $50k+ left per year to account for.
They have given me the faculty scholarship ($22k/year) plus $5k middle income scholarship... the rest of it is loans.

key_points: Neeley School of Business is consistently praised and compared favorably to SMU Cox
STEM and engineering programs seen as weak compared to UT Austin or A&M
One CS professor called out specifically for not answering emails or using slides
key_quotes: Anything BUT improve their STEM programs... LOL.
TCU is a phenomenal school for finance. You'll largely see the same companies recruiting at both TCU and SMU.
Dick Rinewalt doesn't answer emails sometimes and doesn't use slides.

key_points: Campus master plan and rapid enrollment growth raise concerns about quality dilution
Free speech controversy surfaced but current students say conservative speech is not suppressed
TCU shut down The Pub on campus, angering some students
key_quotes: TCU needs to focus on making acceptance rate more competitive… I don't want to go to a university with a 92% acceptance rate.
TCU shut down The Pub, the school can get fucked as far as I'm concerned.
As a current TCU student I can assure you that they do not suppress conservative Christian speech.

key_points: Campus is universally described as beautiful — frequently compared to Vanderbilt
Fort Worth is a real city with strong restaurant and cultural scene nearby
Parking near campus is a genuine problem, especially on game days
key_quotes: TCU and Vanderbilt's campuses look similar.
Parking for an extra 1000 people? Hahahaha.
The campus is beautiful and I was offered generous financial aid.

key_points: Finance and business grads see strong DFW and some West Coast recruiting
Neeley school has notable alumni in finance; Desmond Bane in NBA is a sports highlight
STEM and engineering outcomes not well-represented in student discussions
key_quotes: TCU has a little more presence on the West Coast, and you'll largely see the same companies recruiting at both TCU and SMU.
Desmond Bane (TCU '20) just became the centerpiece of Orlando's championship push.
TCU's name recognition and relationships with local businesses in Dallas and Fort Worth.

key_points: Sticker price around $87k/year all-in — only worth it with substantial merit aid
Students consistently told to choose TCU over SMU only because of scholarship money
Without aid, UNT at $16k/year vs TCU at $87k/year is a stark and repeated comparison
key_quotes: Go to school for as little debt as possible.
Unless you were talking about Wharton or MIT, it's not worth having debt for a normal person.
My dream school is TCU and I got into UNT. TCU is $87k/year after aid, UNT is $16k/year.

key_points: Warm, social, beautiful campus with a strong sports culture and loyal fanbase
Predominantly white, wealthy, Greek-dominated — diversity is limited, especially for Koreans and Asians
Students who get good aid love it; those paying full price often regret it
key_quotes: I graduated from TCU and had the time of my life there. The campus culture promoted study-life balance.
The Korean community at TCU is hella lowkey, prob due to there not being a lot of Koreans.
TCU is a gem of a school.

key_points: **Financial Aid:** International students rejected within 24 hours based on parent income forms
**Diversity:** Korean and Asian students report very small community and limited cultural orgs
**STEM:** Engineering and CS programs consistently called out as weak relative to peer schools
key_quotes: I received my decision less than 24 hours after my mom filled out a form asking how much she would contribute.
I don't think I personally knew any Korean people at TCU, and I had a somewhat diverse friend group.
Anything BUT improve their STEM programs... LOL.

key_points: TCU for Texans program offers free tuition and housing for eligible low-income Texas students
Anonymous donor paid full spring tuition for entire TCU med school graduating class
BFA acting program is genuinely well-regarded; one student chose it over engineering programs at UIUC, Purdue, and Clemson
key_quotes: TCU Announces Free Tuition and Housing for Eligible Texas Students.
Anonymous Donor Pays Spring Tuition for TCU Med School Students.
I decided to turn down UIUC, Purdue, Wisconsin, Texas A&M, and Clemson for a chance to attend TCU.
